hero.subtitle: ISTSDC Ke-V & Symposium Nasional Ke-XXIX
hero.theme: "Advancing Sustainable Mobility Solutions for Resilient and Inclusive Future"

about.label: About
about.title: What is
about.description: Forum Studi Transportasi antar Perguruan Tinggi (FSTPT) is a scientific forum established to foster interaction and communication among students, lecturers, and researchers from universities and institutions across Indonesia engaged in education, research, and community service in the transportation sector. FSTPT is realized through: (1) a mid-year national transportation seminar, (2) an end-of-year national transportation symposium, and (3) the International Symposium on Transportation and Sustainable Development Conference (ISTSDC), held biennially. These platforms serve as avenues for exchanging research findings, scientific studies, and professional experiences to strengthen the Tridharma of Higher Education and support national transportation research and innovation programs. Since its inception in 1998, the end-of-year symposium alone has featured nearly 6,000 presentations by students and lecturers in transportation sciences, while ISTSDC further expands international collaboration and knowledge exchange in sustainable transport development.
about.withTheme: with the theme "Advancing Sustainable Mobility Solutions for Resilient and Inclusive Future"

objectives.title: OBJECTIVES
objectives.subtitle: This symposium is designed with three main, mutually supportive objectives to advance transportation research in Indonesia.

items.title: Sustainable & Resilient Solutions
items.desc: Formulate sustainable, resilient, and inclusive transportation solutions that address the geographical challenges of West Sumatra—mountainous terrain and disaster-prone areas—ensuring safety, accessibility, and long-term sustainability.

items.title: Innovation in Infrastructure
items.desc: Promote innovation in road planning and multimodal infrastructure that adapts to difficult terrain while supporting energy efficiency, digitalization, and the adoption of environmentally friendly technologies.

items.title: Strengthening Collaboration
items.desc: Strengthen collaboration among academics, practitioners, and policymakers to design resilient transportation systems and produce technical recommendations and policy insights relevant to sustainable mobility development in Indonesia and the Southeast Asian region.

activities.title: MAIN ACTIVITIES
activities.subtitle: A series of activities designed to provide the best academic and professional experience for all participants.
activities.block1Desc: The Vth International Symposium on Transportation, Sustainable Development, and Civil Engineering (ISTSDC) combined with the XXIXth National Symposium of FSTPT will feature a series of international and national symposium sessions, including plenary and parallel discussions. These sessions will present keynote speeches and research papers under the theme "Advancing Sustainable Mobility Solutions for Resilient and Inclusive Future", covering 18 sub-themes such as sustainable transport policy, digitalization, road safety, inclusive public transport, green vehicle innovation, and resilient multimodal infrastructure.
activities.block2Desc: In addition to the symposium, the Annual Members' Meeting (Rapat Anggota Akhir Tahun) of FSTPT will be held as a formal gathering to evaluate yearly activities, strengthen organizational networks, and plan future collaborative initiatives. This meeting will serve as a strategic forum to align academic and professional contributions toward advancing sustainable transportation in Indonesia.
activities.block3Desc: The program will also include workshops and training sessions designed to provide hands-on knowledge and practical skills. These workshops will focus on disaster-resilient road planning, sustainable mobility innovations, and the application of digital tools for transport analysis. Through interactive learning, participants will enhance their capacity to address the challenges of disaster-prone regions and difficult terrains, particularly in West Sumatra.
activities.detailSubtitle: Advancing Sustainable Mobility Solutions for Resilient and Inclusive Future
activities.detailP1: The 5th International Symposium on Transportation, Sustainable Development, and Civil Engineering (ISTSDC) together with the 29th National Symposium of FSTPT carries the theme "Advancing Sustainable Mobility Solutions for Resilient and Inclusive Future" as a commitment of academics, practitioners, and policymakers to drive the transformation of transportation systems that are efficient, low-carbon, safe, and sustainable.
activities.detailP2: With 18 sub-themes covering policy, digitalization, road safety, inclusive public transport, green vehicle innovation, and the strengthening of land, sea, air, and railway infrastructure, this forum serves as a collaborative platform across disciplines and across nations to formulate sustainable mobility solutions.
activities.detailP3: Universitas Andalas, particularly the Department of Civil Engineering, has consistently highlighted disaster risk reduction and sustainability issues through various national and international conferences. The organization of the 5th ISTSDC combined with the 29th FSTPT Symposium is expected to further strengthen networks among academics, practitioners, and stakeholders at both national and international levels, while producing technical recommendations and policy insights relevant to the development of sustainable transportation in Indonesia and the Southeast Asian region.

subthemes.label: Theme
subthemes.title: Sub-Themes
subthemes.subtitle: 18 sub-themes covering various important aspects in sustainable transportation development in Indonesia.

items.title: Sustainable Transport Policy & Governance
items.desc: Transportation policies and governance that promote sustainability, efficiency, and equitable access for all layers of society are essential foundations for realizing sustainable mobility. With sustainability-oriented policies, every community group—both in urban and rural areas—can enjoy fair, safe, and affordable transportation access. This directly supports SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure) through the strengthening of resilient and innovative transportation infrastructure, while encouraging industrial and technological growth. At the same time, inclusive transportation policies contribute to SDG 11 (Sustainable Cities and Communities) by creating livable, efficient, and eco-friendly cities where integrated public transport becomes the backbone of community mobility. Furthermore, good transportation governance cannot function without cross-sector and cross-country collaboration, thereby strengthening SDG 17 (Partnerships for the Goals) through strategic partnerships between government, academia, the private sector, and the community.

items.title: Digitalization, AI & Smart Mobility Systems
items.desc: Data-driven mobility planning and community participation ensure transportation solutions are more inclusive and targeted. This approach supports SDG 4 by opening access to education, SDG 11 by building sustainable cities, and SDG 17 through cross-sector collaboration. Meanwhile, the use of digital technology, IoT, and AI increases efficiency and transportation safety. This strengthens SDG 9 through industrial innovation, supports SDG 11 with efficient smart cities, and strengthens SDG 17 through the integration of global transportation systems.

items.title: Emergency Mobility & Evacuation Strategies
items.desc: Emergency mobility strategies are an important part of a sustainable transportation system as they serve to maintain community safety during disasters or crises. Emergency mobility does not only focus on evacuation but also ensures that access to basic needs is maintained. In the context of SDG 2 (Zero Hunger), this strategy guarantees emergency food distribution to affected areas so that communities are not disconnected from food supplies even if main routes are disrupted. Additionally, this strategy supports SDG 11 (Sustainable Cities and Communities) by strengthening community protection through planned evacuation routes, public transport readiness, and integration with urban spatial planning so that communities are more resilient in facing extreme conditions.

items.title: Inclusive & Accessible Public Transport
items.desc: Public transport that is friendly to vulnerable groups is an essential element in realizing a sustainable mobility system. With universal design that considers the needs of people with disabilities, the elderly, children, and low-income communities, public transport opens wider access to educational services. This is in line with SDG 4 (Quality Education), as safe and affordable transportation access allows students from various backgrounds to reach schools and universities without barriers. Furthermore, inclusive public transport supports SDG 11 (Sustainable Cities and Communities) by creating livable cities where all citizens can actively participate in social, economic, and cultural life.

items.title: Green & Low-Carbon Vehicle Innovations
items.desc: Green vehicle innovations are one of the important pillars in realizing sustainable mobility. The development of electric, hydrogen, and hybrid vehicles not only presents more efficient transportation technology but also encourages the growth of the automotive industry based on clean energy, thereby supporting SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure) through the strengthening of innovation and green infrastructure. At the same time, the implementation of low-emission vehicles directly contributes to SDG 11 (Sustainable Cities and Communities) by creating healthier, more sustainable, and livable cities, as air quality improves and pollution decreases.

items.title: Sustainable Transport as a Driver of Smart Cities
items.desc: Sustainable transportation is the main driving force in realizing efficient, inclusive, and eco-friendly smart cities. By integrating smart technology, clean energy, and connected mobility systems, sustainable transportation strengthens SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure) through the development of innovative infrastructure that supports industrial growth and accelerates the adoption of new technologies. At the same time, the implementation of sustainable transportation contributes to SDG 11 (Sustainable Cities and Communities) by creating more efficient, healthy, and livable cities.

items.title: Freight & Logistics Innovations for Sustainable Supply Chains
items.desc: Logistics innovation is a key factor in strengthening sustainable transportation and distribution systems. With the application of digital technology, automation, and data analysis, food supply chains can be managed more efficiently and sustainably, thereby supporting SDG 2 (Zero Hunger) by ensuring food availability evenly to remote areas. From the perspective of SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure), logistics innovation drives industrial efficiency through reduced operational costs, increased productivity, and the development of transportation infrastructure that is more resilient and adaptive to change.

items.title: Rail Electrification & Smart Railway Systems
items.desc: Rail electrification is an important step in the transformation of sustainable transportation. By switching from fossil fuels to electricity, this system promotes SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure) through the development of modern transportation technology that is eco-friendly and strengthens mass mobility infrastructure. Furthermore, rail electrification supports SDG 11 (Sustainable Cities and Communities) by presenting more efficient, low-emission, and integrated urban mobility, thereby improving air quality and making cities more livable.

items.title: Sustainable & Climate-Resilient Maritime Infrastructure
items.desc: Sustainable ports have a vital role in supporting sustainable development across various sectors. In the context of SDG 2 (Zero Hunger), efficient and eco-friendly ports ensure smooth cross-regional food distribution, allowing food supplies to reach remote areas or other countries more quickly and safely. From the perspective of SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure), the development of modern maritime infrastructure that is adaptive to climate change and based on digital technology strengthens the logistics industry while increasing global competitiveness.

items.title: Digitalization & Smart Maritime Logistics
items.desc: Digital transformation in maritime logistics becomes an important catalyst for more efficient and sustainable global distribution systems. With the application of technologies such as Internet of Things (IoT), big data, and real-time tracking systems, cross-country food distribution can be done more quickly, accurately, and transparently, thereby supporting SDG 2 (Zero Hunger) by maintaining world food security. From the perspective of SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure), digitalization drives maritime industry innovation through automated loading and unloading processes, shipping route optimization, and the development of smart port infrastructure that increases global competitiveness.

items.title: Resilient & Sustainable Road Pavement Systems
items.desc: Resilient road pavements are an essential foundation for sustainable mobility. With construction capable of withstanding traffic loads and extreme climate conditions, strong roads ensure the smooth distribution of food, thereby supporting SDG 2 (Zero Hunger) by keeping supply chains stable. From the perspective of SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure), the application of innovative materials and eco-friendly technology in road construction and maintenance drives industrial efficiency while strengthening transportation infrastructure.

items.title: Sustainable & Climate-Resilient Air Transport
items.desc: Sustainable air transport has a strategic role in supporting cross-sector development. In the context of SDG 2 (Zero Hunger), sustainable aviation allows rapid distribution of emergency food to remote or disaster-affected areas, ensuring that community access to basic needs is maintained. From the perspective of SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure), aviation innovation through the use of green fuels, energy-efficient aircraft, and eco-friendly airports strengthens the transportation industry while promoting climate-friendly technology.

items.title: Freight & Logistics Innovations for Sustainable Supply Chains
items.desc: Logistics innovation plays an important role in strengthening resilient and sustainable distribution systems. With the application of digital technology, automation, and data analysis, food supply chains can be managed more efficiently, thereby supporting SDG 2 (Zero Hunger) by ensuring food availability is distributed evenly even to remote areas. From the perspective of SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure), logistics innovation increases industrial efficiency through cost reduction, productivity increases, and the development of transportation infrastructure that is more adaptive to change.

items.title: Rail Electrification & Smart Railway Systems
items.desc: Rail electrification is a strategic step in realizing modern and efficient sustainable transportation systems. By replacing fossil fuel use with electricity, electric trains promote SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure) through the development of more eco-friendly transportation technology while strengthening mass mobility infrastructure. Furthermore, rail electrification supports SDG 11 (Sustainable Cities and Communities) by presenting more efficient, low-emission, and integrated urban mobility.

items.title: Sustainable & Climate-Resilient Maritime Infrastructure
items.desc: Sustainable ports have a strategic role in supporting cross-sector sustainable development. In the context of SDG 2 (Zero Hunger), resilient and eco-friendly ports ensure smooth cross-regional food distribution, so food supplies can reach remote areas or other countries efficiently. From the perspective of SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure), the development of modern maritime infrastructure, which is adaptive to climate change and based on technology, supports the growth of the logistics industry and strengthens global supply chains.

items.title: Digitalization & Smart Maritime Logistics
items.desc: Digital transformation in maritime logistics plays an important role in improving efficiency, transparency, and global competitiveness. Through the application of digital technology, real-time tracking systems, and data integration between ports and supply chains, food distribution can be done faster and more accurately. This supports SDG 2 (Zero Hunger) by ensuring global food supplies are maintained, even in remote areas or during emergency conditions.

items.title: Resilient & Sustainable Road Pavement Systems
items.desc: Resilient road pavements are an important element in supporting sustainable transportation systems. With designs that are adaptive to traffic loads and extreme climate conditions, strong roads ensure the smooth distribution of food to various regions, thereby supporting SDG 2 (Zero Hunger) by keeping supply chains running without barriers. From the perspective of SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure), the development of innovative eco-friendly materials and the application of preventive preservation techniques strengthen transportation infrastructure and drive the efficiency of the construction industry.

items.title: Sustainable & Climate-Resilient Air Transport
items.desc: Sustainable air transport has a strategic role in supporting cross-sector sustainable development. In the context of SDG 2 (Zero Hunger), sustainable aviation allows the rapid distribution of emergency food to remote or disaster-affected areas, ensuring that the community still has access to basic needs even if land or sea routes are disrupted. From the perspective of SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure), aviation innovation through the use of green fuels, energy-efficient aircraft, and climate-resilient airports strengthens the air transport industry while encouraging the development of eco-friendly technology.

authorGuidelineContent.paperTitle: PAPER WRITING INSTRUCTIONS FOR FSTPT XXIX NATIONAL SYMPOSIUM,
authorGuidelineContent.author1Role: Master Student of Civil Engineering, Faculty of Engineering
authorGuidelineContent.abstractContent: This document consists of detailed paper writing instructions for Seminar Nasional FSTPT XXIX at Andalas University Padang. To submit a paper in this seminar, please follow the following guidelines for writing the paper. The author should read through these instructions before writing a paper. Papers that do not follow these instructions are not included in the seminar. The deadline for submission a draft of full paper is September 25, 2023. The paper will be reviewed by a reviewer team and the results will be informed through the author's email address. All accepted papers will be published in a seminar proceeding that has an ISBN and uploaded online. The best papers and presentations in the seminar will be awarded by the committee. The abstract is written in a concise and straightforward sentence and must not more than 250 words.
authorGuidelineContent.keywords: Keywords: keyword 1, keyword 2, keyword 3, keyword 4, keyword 5
authorGuidelineContent.abstrakContent: This document consists of detailed instructions for paper writing for ACE Conference 2023 at Andalas University Padang. To submit a paper in this seminar, please follow the writing instructions below. Authors must read all these instructions before writing a paper. Papers that do not follow these instructions will not be included in the seminar. The deadline for submission of a complete draft paper is September 25, 2023. Papers will be reviewed by a reviewer team and results will be informed through the author's email address. All accepted papers will be published in seminar proceedings with an ISBN and uploaded online. The best papers and presentations will be awarded by the committee. The abstract should be written in concise and straightforward sentences of no more than 250 words.
authorGuidelineContent.abstrakKeywords: Keywords: keyword 1, keyword 2, keyword 3, keyword 4, keyword 5
authorGuidelineContent.section1P1: These instructions are made as a guide for preparing papers suitable for presentation in the seminar. The first author is the researcher, master's or doctoral student, while subsequent authors can be supervisors or co-supervisors or colleagues in the research group.
authorGuidelineContent.section1P2: Authors must write their papers according to these instructions so that the paper can be accepted. The submitted paper must be written using
authorGuidelineContent.section2_1Intro: The paper must follow the following format:
authorGuidelineContent.format2: Margin: Top 3 cm, Bottom 4 cm, Left 3 cm, and Right 3 cm
authorGuidelineContent.format3: Font type is Times New Roman
authorGuidelineContent.format5: Text writing: single spaced and single column text
authorGuidelineContent.format6: Writing in headers and footers is prohibited
authorGuidelineContent.formatNote: * Please recheck for any typos and spelling errors. Follow the format settings using the Ms-Word styles provided in the template.
authorGuidelineContent.section2_2Intro: In this paper file, Ms-Word styles have been provided for formatting the paper. Authors simply need to click the corresponding style to get proper paper formatting.
authorGuidelineContent.styleTitleDesc: Font size Times New Roman 16pt, bold, single spaced.
authorGuidelineContent.styleAuthorDesc: Author names written in one line, add superscript numbers for affiliation identification.
authorGuidelineContent.styleAffiliationDesc: Department/Study Program, Faculty, University, and Email information.
authorGuidelineContent.styleAbstractDesc: Abstract – header for title and Abstract for content (single column, single paragraph).
authorGuidelineContent.styleKeyword: Provide a maximum of five keywords using the style
authorGuidelineContent.styleKeywordAfter: . Bold the word 'Abstract:' before the keywords.
authorGuidelineContent.styleH3After: . Chapter numbers will be automatically generated by Ms-Word.
authorGuidelineContent.section2_3P1: Equations, tables, and figures must be placed between the text at the appropriate location for discussion of the table and figure. Tables must be numbered sequentially. Table titles are placed above the table.
authorGuidelineContent.tableCaption: Table 1. Service conditions of travel attributes
authorGuidelineContent.section2_3P2: Figures must be numbered sequentially. Figure titles are placed below the figure. Figures must be clear and easy to read. See Figure 1, equations must also be numbered sequentially. Numbers are placed on the right side of the equation.
authorGuidelineContent.figureCaption: Figure 1. Paper selection diagram at ACE 8
authorGuidelineContent.section3Intro: The paper structure should consist of the following main components:
authorGuidelineContent.section4P1: The bibliography should only contain references cited in the paper text. This bibliography is written in ascending order based on the last name of the author. Use the style
authorGuidelineContent.section4Format: Reference writing format follows the APA 6th method, the example of writing is as follows: Author name. Year of publication. Publication title. Place of publication: publisher.
authorGuidelineContent.section5P1: If needed, add acknowledgments to parties who helped in the research, paper writing, or seminar execution.
authorGuidelineContent.section6P1: The paper can be supplemented with appendices containing tables, figures, maps, and others if necessary to be attached. Appendices must comply with language requirements, page numbering, header, page format, table and figure format.
